articles.title: The UK Solicitor's Guide to Compliant Call Recording in 2026
articles.body:
Call recording for UK solicitors is a compliance minefield. Get it right and you have an unassailable evidence trail. Get it wrong and you face SRA sanctions, GDPR fines, and malpractice claims.

After deploying compliant recording systems for 30 UK law firms, here is the definitive guide.

## When Must You Record?

| Scenario | Recording Required? | Legal Basis |
|----------|-------------------|-------------|
| Client instructions received by phone | Strongly recommended | Professional duty to document |
| Settlement negotiations | Strongly recommended | Evidence of agreed terms |
| Witness statements by phone | Required if used as evidence | CPR Part 32 |
| Calls with opposing counsel | Check with SRA | May be prohibited without disclosure |
| Internal calls between solicitors | Not required | But useful for training |
| Calls involving financial transactions | Required (if FCA regulated) | MiFID II / FCA SYSC 10A |

## The SRA Position

The SRA does not mandate call recording. However, SRA Principle 7 requires solicitors to act in the best interests of clients, and Principle 4 requires acting with honesty. Call recordings provide evidence of compliance with both.

**Practical translation:** If a client disputes what was said on a call and you have no recording, you are in a weaker position than if you had one.

## GDPR Compliance for Call Recording

### Legal Basis

For UK solicitors, **legitimate interest** is the strongest legal basis for recording:

| Element | How to Satisfy |
|---------|---------------|
| Purpose | Quality assurance, dispute resolution, training, compliance |
| Necessity | Cannot achieve purpose without recording (notes are incomplete) |
| Balance | Client benefit (accurate records) outweighs privacy impact |
| LIA documented | Written Legitimate Interest Assessment on file |

### Announcement

Play a recording announcement at the start of every call: "This call may be recorded for quality, training, and compliance purposes."

This is notification, not consent. Under legitimate interest, you do not need consent — but you must notify.

### Retention

| Matter Type | Recommended Retention | Basis |
|------------|----------------------|-------|
| Conveyancing | 6 years after completion | Limitation Act 1980 |
| Litigation | 6 years after matter closes | Limitation Act 1980 |
| Family law | 6 years after final order | Limitation Act 1980 |
| Criminal | 6 years after case concludes | Limitation Act 1980 |
| Wills and probate | 12 years (or indefinitely) | Long-stop limitation |

### Access Controls

| Who | Access Level |
|-----|-------------|
| Fee earner handling the matter | Full access to matter recordings |
| Supervising partner | Full access to department recordings |
| COLP/COFA | Full access for compliance review |
| IT administrator | No access to recording content |
| Other staff | No access |

## Subject Access Requests (SARs)

Clients can request copies of their call recordings under Article 15. You must:
1. Respond within 30 days
2. Provide recordings in accessible format (MP3)
3. Redact any third-party personal data in the recording
4. Not charge a fee (unless manifestly excessive)

**Practical tip:** Use a VoIP system with searchable recordings. If a SAR arrives and you have to manually search 10,000 recordings, you will miss the 30-day deadline.

## What Your Phone System Needs

| Requirement | Why |
|------------|-----|
| Automatic recording (all calls) | Manual recording leads to inconsistency |
| AES-256 encryption at rest | Data protection requirement |
| Role-based access | Only authorised people access recordings |
| Immutable audit log | Who accessed which recording, when |
| Search by phone number/date | SAR compliance (30-day deadline) |
| Export in standard format | MP3/WAV for disclosure |
| Configurable retention | Auto-delete after retention period |
| Recording announcement | Plays before agent connects |

[DialPhone](https://dialphone.com) provides all 8 requirements in their standard plan at £24/user/month. No add-on charges for recording, encryption, or access controls. Their system is deployed in 30+ UK law firms with full SRA and GDPR compliance documentation available on request.

articles.title: How DialPhone Reduced a 50-Person Company's Phone Bill from £3,800 to £1,200 in One Weekend
articles.body:
A 50-person digital marketing agency in Manchester was paying £3,800 per month for their phone system. Two PRI circuits, a Mitel maintenance contract, and 14 separate feature add-ons.

We migrated them to [DialPhone](https://dialphone.com) over a single weekend. Here is the minute-by-minute account.

## Friday 4 PM: The Starting Position

| Line Item | Monthly Cost |
|-----------|-------------|
| 2 PRI circuits (46 channels) | £1,380 |
| Mitel maintenance | £520 |
| Call recording (50 users) | £375 |
| Voicemail (50 users) | £200 |
| Auto-attendant | £65 |
| Conference bridge | £95 |
| Mobile twinning (20 users) | £90 |
| Long distance | £280 |
| International (US clients) | £190 |
| Analytics add-on | £150 |
| CRM connector (HubSpot) | £200 |
| IT contractor (phone admin) | £400 |
| **Total** | **£3,945** |

Peak concurrent calls from CDR data: 16. They were paying for 46 channels.

## Friday 5 PM: System Configuration

DialPhone migration specialist Sarah started configuring:
- 50 user accounts created
- 4 ring groups (sales, account management, creative, operations)
- Auto-attendant with 4 menu options
- Call recording enabled for all users
- HubSpot integration connected
- After-hours routing to senior management mobiles

Time: 2 hours. We reviewed at 7 PM. Two changes: added the MD to the sales overflow group, changed the hold music.

## Saturday 9 AM: Phone Deployment

15 desk phones (reception, meeting rooms, operations) arrived pre-configured. Plugged in, auto-provisioned in 90 seconds each.

35 softphone users received email invitations at 9:30 AM. Desktop app + mobile app. Most installed within the hour.

By 11 AM: all 50 users operational on temporary numbers.

## Saturday 12 PM: Testing

| Test | Result |
|------|--------|
| Inbound to main temp number | All 4 ring groups rang correctly |
| Auto-attendant menu | All options routed correctly |
| Transfer between departments | Clean, no drops |
| Mobile app inbound | 10/10 calls rang successfully |
| HubSpot screen pop | Contact record appeared in < 2 seconds |
| Call recording | Playback working, searchable |
| International (US test call) | Clear audio, correct caller ID |

Issues found: zero.

## Saturday 3 PM: Number Port Submitted

Port request for 8 numbers submitted. Estimated completion: Wednesday.

## Monday 8 AM: Staff Training

30-minute video session. Key questions:
- "Can I use the mobile app and desk phone at the same time?" (Yes)
- "How do I transfer to someone's voicemail?" (Press transfer, dial *ext)
- "Will my HubSpot notes appear on screen?" (Yes, automatically)

## Wednesday 6 AM: Port Completed

All 8 numbers switched to DialPhone overnight. First call at 7:15 AM. Zero issues.

## The New Monthly Cost

| Item | Cost |
|------|------|
| 50 users × £24/month | £1,200 |
| Everything included | £0 extra |
| **Total** | **£1,200** |

## The Savings

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Old monthly cost | £3,945 |
| New monthly cost | £1,200 |
| Monthly savings | £2,745 |
| Annual savings | £32,940 |
| Hardware cost (15 phones) | £2,250 |
| Break-even | 24 days |
| 3-year net savings | £96,570 |

And they gained: mobile app for 50 users (previously 20), HubSpot integration (previously £200/month add-on), unlimited international to US (previously £190/month), and analytics (previously £150/month add-on).

They paid less and got more. That is not marketing — it is maths.

For your own savings calculation, visit [DialPhone](https://dialphone.com) and request a free bill analysis.
